blocked_by_gate_questions.question: Read public marketplace/category pages to compare demand signals, saturation, price bands, and buyer language.
Review seller terms, tax/KYC/payment setup, refund obligations, and platform fees.
Create, publish, update, or promote a marketplace listing or public product page.
Create seller accounts, connect payouts, accept agreements, or configure payment settings.

candidates.buyer: Solo AI builders and operators preparing small launches.
candidates.local_reason: Matches the lane evidence around reusable digital assets without requiring platform-specific integrations.
candidates.needs_live_validation: Marketplace demand, price bands, saturation, and buyer wording remain behind browser read-only approval.
candidates.promise: Reduce launch setup friction with reusable checklist, positioning, and QA templates.

candidates.buyer: Small agencies that need repeatable client onboarding and project intake.
candidates.local_reason: Reusable operational assets have a clear time-saved angle and can be drafted locally.
candidates.needs_live_validation: Seller terms, refund obligations, and support expectations remain behind legal/payment review.
candidates.promise: Turn a messy intake flow into a repeatable prompt, form, and handoff bundle.

candidates.buyer: Creators tracking sponsors, deliverables, invoices, and renewal follow-ups.
candidates.local_reason: Narrow, low-integration product that can be built and screenshotted locally before any listing.
candidates.needs_live_validation: Public listing, pricing, and platform fit remain gated until marketplace/browser approval.
candidates.promise: Give small creators one lightweight operating sheet for sponsor workflow control.

forbidden_actions: Do not browse marketplace pages, create seller accounts, accept terms, publish listings, or configure payouts from this memo.
Do not mutate service requests, assign/start workers, call APIs, touch wallets/payments, trade, submit, post, or create external side effects from this memo.

memo_sections.summary: Local evidence supports buyers who value reusable templates/plugins that save setup or coordination time.
Three local candidate product shapes are ready for a build brief, but none has live marketplace validation yet.
Each candidate should promise time saved, fewer setup mistakes, or clearer handoff rather than broad passive-income language.
Local validation can draft screenshots, contents, support assumptions, and buyer copy before any marketplace browsing.
Marketplace research, seller terms, account setup, payouts, and public listings remain parked behind explicit gates.
Prepare a build brief locally; do not browse marketplaces, list, publish, or configure payment.

next_action: Prepare a local build brief for the strongest candidate; request marketplace/browser approval only if live demand comparison is needed.
summary: Converted the local digital-products demand proof into a local demand memo with three candidate product shapes, six decision sections, and the same live-marketplace/legal/payment gates preserved.
